channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.ign.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.ign.com

item.title: Square Enix Games Are Getting Hefty Discounts in Amazon's Prime Day Sales
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix is one of the most beloved developers in the game industry for a reason. The Japanese publisher is known for iconic series like Final Fantasy, Dragon Quest, Nier, and many more. Amazon Prime Day is now officially live and includes some very solid Square Enix deals. If you've missed out on some of the company's biggest games in recent years, this is a great opportunity to pick up your next RPG at a discount.

Save on Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade

FINAL FANTASY VII REMAKE INTERGRADE - Nintendo Switch 2 (Standard Edition)

FINAL FANTASY VII REMAKE INTERGRADE

Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade is the ultimate version of the first chapter in the Final Fantasy VII remake series, following Cloud Strife and the party from the very beginning through the escape from Midgar. This vastly expanded adventure is solely set in Midgar, allowing you to connect with the city like never before. Intergrade brought graphical enhancements and two new chapters, called Episode Intermission, that star Yuffie Kisaragi and her infiltration of Shinra HQ.

For Prime Day, you can score Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade for $29.99 on the Nintendo Switch 2 and $19.99 on the PlayStation 5. If you want to prepare for Final Fantasy VII Revelation next year, this is where to start your journey. I've played both versions extensively, and rest assured, each is an excellent way to experience Intergrade.

Dragon Quest VII Reimagined for $44.99

Dragon Quest VII Reimagined just released in February, and if you've yet to pick up the game, you can save $15 buying it for any platform during Prime Day. This adventure reimagines the 2000 classic with 3D-scanned physical dolls to create a unique, almost diorama-like art style. In our 7/10 review, we wrote, "Every version of Dragon Quest VII is a flawed gem, with its own blemishes and imperfections, but gems they all remain. The way it reveals its world to you stands out from any other game in this long-running series, and seeing the results of your actions in the past affect the present day is just as charming now as it was when I first put the disk in my PlayStation."

Octopath Traveler 0 for $34.99

OCTOPATH TRAVELER 0 (PS5)

OCTOPATH TRAVELER 0 (Switch 2)

I don't think it is a stretch to call Octopath Traveler 0 one of the best RPGs of 2025. This game is jam-packed with content, with Square Enix reimagining some of the storyline from Champions of the Continent as a single-player experience on consoles. During Prime Day, you can pick up a copy for $34.99, a steal considering this game easily has over 100 hours of content. Octopath Traveler is a standalone game, so you can jump in and experience this RPG even if its your first time checking out the series.

Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake for $39.99

DRAGON QUEST I & II HD-2D Remake (PlayStation 5)

DRAGON QUEST I & II HD-2D Remake (Nintendo Switch 2)

Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake is a very ambitious pair of remakes, breathing new life into these classics. Dragon Quest II especially saw huge additions to its story, making this the definitive version of the game. Amazon has discounted copies of Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake down to $39.99, which is one of the best prices we've seen since release. In our 9/10 review, we wrote, "Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake beautifully concludes this trilogy, recapturing the retro magic of the originals while giving them a modern facelift."

Dragon Quest III HD-2D Remake for $24.99

DRAGON QUEST III HD-2D Remake (NSW)

DRAGON QUEST III HD-2D Remake (PS5)

Dragon Quest III HD-2D Remake was such a joy to experience in 2024, as it reimagined one of the most legendary RPGs ever in a brilliant way. For Prime Day, you can score a copy for only $24.99. It's a surprisingly meaty experience, with the 100% completion taking around 70 hours in my experience. This is an excellent game to pack and play on the go on the Nintendo Switch, and you can even take advantage of a free update for the Switch 2 to experience enhanced resolution and frame rate.

Pre-Order Upcoming Square Enix Games

Finally, upcoming Square Enix games are also available to pre-order. This includes the Kingdom Hearts Collection [I-III] for the Switch 2 and PS5, which packs in every Kingdom Hearts game besides Melody of Memory. Others up for preorder are Final Fantasy Resonance, the first HD-2D Final Fantasy, as well as the next two Dragon Quest releases.

Noah Hunter is a freelance writer and reviewer with a passion for games and technology. He co-founded Final Weapon, an outlet focused on nonsense-free Japanese gaming (in 2019) and has contributed to various publishers writing about the medium.



https://www.ign.com/articles/square-enix-game-deals-amazon-prime-day-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Fox Releases on Switch 2 Tomorrow — Don’t Miss Out on $10 Off Preorders
item.content:encoded:
Star Fox on Nintendo Switch 2 is officially out tomorrow, June 25, and if you haven't yet secured a copy to boot up on launch day, now is the time to do it. Both Amazon and Walmart still have physical versions marked down to $49.94, offering a $10 cut from the full $59.99 price, but there's no telling if that discount will last after the game is out in the wild (though it would be cool if it did).

If you've been hoping to take this remake for a spin tomorrow, don't miss out on this chance to save $10 on a physical copy of it before then.

Star Fox

This preorder discount was one of our favorite surprises recently, as it followed the news that Nintendo was changing the pricing between physical and digital Switch 2 games so the former cost more. It's not the only Switch 2 game to get $10 off, either. We broke down every Switch 2 physical preorder that's on sale right now, so you can pick up your favorites while they're still discounted.

If you're curious what this remake of Star Fox 64 has in store, our review from Jada Griffin said it "proves the Arwing isn’t out of style quite yet." Awarding it an 8/10, Griffin remarked that, "Despite knowing nearly all Star Fox 64’s shortcuts and secrets for close to 30 years now, this remake’s impressive coat of paint and compelling new cutscenes made me smile like I just did my very first barrel roll."

If you're looking for even more deals to shop right now, you're in luck: Amazon's Prime Day sale has entered into its second day (it's a four-day-long event this year), and it's chock-full of exciting offers to check out. If you're a Prime member hoping to score some more games on sale, have a look at our rundown of the 35 best gaming deals in the Prime Day sale to see some of our top picks.

Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.



https://www.ign.com/articles/star-fox-on-switch-2-releases-tomorrow-dont-miss-out-on-10-off-its-preorder
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Begins Reselling Old Battle Pass Skins in Next Update
item.content:encoded:
Absolute Doom for your wallet.

Fortnite will begin reselling content from old battle passes as part of its next update.

The scheme will start off with skins from Chapter 5 Season 4: Absolute Doom, which was Fortnite's most recent Marvel-dedicated season that ran from August 16 to November 2, 2024.

This included Marvel characters such as Doctor Doom, Mysterio, Shuri, War Machine, Gwenpool and Emma Frost, as well as a Captain America version of Fortnite character Jonesy. All are now expected to imminently return via Fortnite's in-game shop.

Fortnite previously pledged to never resell content released as part of its time-limited battle passes, a policy that made them must-have purchases at the time, but ultimately served to only lock newer players out of characters released years ago.

As Fortnite edges closer to its 10th birthday, Epic Games decided to drop this policy from 2024 onwards, beginning with the Ultimate Doom season — though still said that battle pass content would not be sold separately for 18 months. (And, of course, the price of a season pass remains far less than the cost of buying all of its characters separately, with battle passes selling for around $10 — about the same price in V-Bucks for some individual skins.)

Of course, that 18 month time period has now lapsed for the Absolute Doom season — and fans had been wondering when it would reappear. Now, in a teaser released via X/Twitter, Epic Games has shown the vault doors opening back to this content, which fans have reacted positively towards.

While a major change in policy, the practice of selling newer battle pass content is likely a win-win for Epic Games. It gets to re-monetize characters individually at a huge markup, and players don't feel FOMO to buy battle passes at the time.

It's a typically busy time for Fortnite, with its next update rolling out tomorrow loaded with additions, before Epic Games departs for its annual two-week summer break. Other content coming to the game over the next fortnight includes a major collaboration with popstar Olivia Rodrigo, and a crossover with Neopets.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

Recommends

In This Article
